rows.notes: Top-of-page title. Both words are clearly written in large script and unambiguous.
Centered section header, underlined. Text is clear and introduces the group of asset entries below.
Numbers align clearly in the pounds/shillings/pence columns (pounds = '3600', shillings = '18', pence = '6'). Vertical ruling lines and the numerals are distinct.
Abbreviated 'do' (ditto) with parenthetical note. Amount clearly reads 65 pounds, 13 shillings, 4 pence; alignment with column lines is unambiguous.
Underlined subtotal at right of the two Cash lines. Numerals '3666 11 10' are neatly written and align with the pounds/s/shillings/pence columns.

Pounds clearly read as '3000' (there is a small mark after the number). No shillings/pence are written (appears blank or indicated by a dot). I left shillings/pence empty rather than assuming zeros because the original shows no explicit figures in those columns.
Clear entry: '5303 6 6' aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. All digits distinct and within valid ranges.
Underlined subtotal for Furniture + Investments shown as '8303 6 6'. Clear alignment with the vertical ruling lines; values validate (shillings 0–19, pence 0–11).
Section header (introduces the following list of receipts). The dash/colon after 'College' is visible; text is legible.
Amount reads '242 10 .' with a dot in the pence position indicating zero pence. Pounds/shillings alignment is clear.

Digits read as '444 7 5'. The hundreds-digit grouping is somewhat cramped but the three-digit pounds figure appears to be '444' (loops on the 4s are slightly stylized). Column alignment supports this reading.

Small amount in narrow handwriting appears as '1 8 2'. The first digit may be '1' (looks like a single stroke) but could be read as part of a larger digit if ink crowded — chosen '1' based on alignment with the pounds column and the visible stroke spacing.
Clear reading '53 13 4' with proper alignment in the three currency columns; handwriting is neat and unambiguous.
Reads '3 5 .' with a dot indicating zero pence. Pound and shilling digits are small but align with the ruling lines; pence blank interpreted as zero.
Clear numeric entry '36 11 3'. The 'Dr' and name are legible; the amounts are well aligned and distinct.

Description handwriting slightly cramped (reads as 'Furniture Hic' or similar), amounts '17 6 1' are legible and in the correct columns; small uncertainty only in the trailing text of the description.
Reads '3 8 4'. Small neat figures; clear column alignment. Slightly lower than 1.0 only because the entry is short and written in smaller script, but digits are readable.
Underlined subtotal for the 'Due to the College' group, clearly written as '771 19 7' at the right. Characters align exactly with the pounds/s/pence columns.
Final boxed/underlined grand total shown with a leading currency symbol and the figures '12741 17 11'. Very clear and prominently written, matching column alignment.

rows.description: duly kept in proper books of Account and that the account of the financial condition of the College.
rows.notes: Handwritten certification sentence at bottom left. The wording is legible though written in cursive; slight uncertainty on a couple of small words but overall phrase is readable.

rows.description: W. C. Jackson F.C.A. auditors (Jackson Pooley Browning Harry & Co)
rows.notes: Signature block in cursive. The signature 'W. C. Jackson' and 'F.C.A. auditors' are fairly clear; the parenthetical firm name is partially obscured/stylized and several letters are uncertain (transcribed to best visual reading).
Date written near the signature. Numerals and month abbreviation are clear; minor ambiguity in punctuation only.
